text: This book offers the first comparative history of the political, military and economic aspects of the Soviet occupation of Austria, Hungary and Romania. Drawing on a wide range of primary sources in five languages, including materials from the Austrian, Hungarian, Romanian and Russian archives, the authors of the twelve chapters not only challenge recent interpretations of Soviet behaviour in the occupied countries but also illuminate in unprecedented detail the motivations of Stalin and other Soviet leaders. A new perspective on Stalin's conquest of Eastern Europe is provided, highlighting the imperial and economic dimensions of his policies. This involved transforming the conquered countries into regions of Soviet military and economic power. Finally, the volume casts a light into some of the darkest corners of post-war history, such as massacres, looting and deportations, and highlights the awful experiences of people living under Soviet occupation.
